new_sources_to_consider.rationale: Nathaniel Herz's independent Alaska-focused outlet; broke the Stak Energy North Slope data center story before syndication.
Veteran Alaska journalist Dermot Cole's Substack; carried the only in-window analysis of the North Slope Power / Crum pension angle (single-source, dropped this week but worth monitoring).
Fairbanks Daily News-Miner; consistent Interior/military beat coverage relevant to UAF and Eielson stories.
Copper River Country Journal; cross-publishes Alaska Beacon when alaskabeacon.com is paywall/Cloudflare-blocked.
KNOM Nome — Western Alaska coverage of ANSEP, Bering Straits Native Corporation, and rural tech/education stories underrepresented in current seed list.
UAF student newspaper; first to publish on Alaska Native Language Center grant-termination depositions.
Original coverage of NOAA AFSC AI tooling (YOLOv11 salmon excluder, pollock bycatch tracking) not replicated by Alaska outlets.

stories_dropped_in_validation.reason: Single source (Dermot Cole), primary_source: false. Crum / I Squared Capital / North Slope Power material not used in this week's post.

background_context_only.note: ADN May 12 reader letter opposing Mat-Su / Terra Energy coal plant; reader letter, weak source.
ANSEP Nome Academy inaugural year completed May 14; no AI component.
May 7 McMahon ruling on NEH/DOGE ChatGPT grant terminations — lead frame of last week's post.
UAF/DIU MOU signed May 8 — covered in last week's post.
